title: Pepinia corallina
intro: Pepinia corallina, also known as Maypop or Wild Passionflower, is a perennial climbing vine native to the southeastern United States. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating anxiety, insomnia, and seizures due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Pepinia corallina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pepinia corallina are teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Pepinia corallina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is native to Central and South America. Within the Annonaceae family, Pepinia corallina is placed in the tribe Annoneae and subfamily Annonoideae. Further classification includes its genus, Pepinia, and its species, P. corallina. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Annonaceae family.
